sub_mechanisms: M_1: enumerate the index space of all valid set-partitions of N tokens into K experts
M_2: select a specific partition by an input-derived index (e.g., context-window hash mod S(N,K))
M_3: enforce balanced cell sizes (constrain to partitions whose largest cell minus smallest cell ≤ epsilon)
M_4: map cells to experts deterministically under fixed seed
stripped_llm_application: A deterministic mixture-of-experts router that uses an indexed enumeration of set-partitions to select a specific partition of N tokens into K non-empty expert groups; the index is derived from input; the partition is constrained to be balanced; the mapping from cells to experts is fixed under seed.
novelty_claim_stripped: Top-K routing produces probabilistic load distributions. Sequence-level routing assigns all tokens of a sequence to one expert. Stirling-indexed routing enumerates set-partitions and selects deterministically — eliminates routing variance while preserving multi-expert utilization.
